item.title: Amazon Prime Day Pizza Promotion Offers Clue to Meal-Delivery Task
item.description: Online retailer hasn’t had success in the competitive dinner-delivery market

item.title: China Warns of Trade Fractures If Protectionism Persists
item.description: The US and EU have sought to diversify away from China, which continues to export more to both economies than it imports

item.title: Andy Burnham Might Shift the UK’s Fiscal Goalposts Again
item.description: Keir Starmer quitting may lead to a new set of UK fiscal rules.

item.title: Portugal Eyes Wealth Fund for Strategic Stakes, Premier Says
item.description: The Portuguese government plans to create a sovereign wealth fund that would allow the state to retain stakes in strategic sectors of the economy.

item.title: China Makes Sweeping Education Reforms to Prepare for AI Era
item.description: Beijing has eliminated over 12,000 university degree programs, dismissing many as relics of a bygone era

item.title: ‘Buy Europe’ Trade Returns as Stagflation Fears Ease: Markets Daily
item.description: Bets on easing inflation and stronger economic growth are boosting the region’s stocks.

item.title: JPMorgan’s Lipikhina Sees Europe Stocks Lagging Despite Oil Drop
item.description: European equities may struggle to keep pace with US and emerging market peers in the second half of the year as falling oil prices threaten a key source of earnings growth, according to Nataliia Lipikhina at JPMorgan Chase & Co.

item.title: Microsoft and Chevron Sign 20-Year Power Deal For Texas Data Center
item.description: Chevron Corp. signed a 20-year deal with Microsoft Corp. to provide natural-gas fired power for a proposed West Texas data center, which could be one of the biggest in the US.

item.title: Getty Images Soars 200% in Early Trading After OpenAI Deal
item.description: Shares of Getty Images Holdings Inc. jumped about 200% in premarket trading on Monday after the photography repository announced a licensing deal with OpenAI.

item.title: China Lays Out Blueprint to Boost Consumption of Clean Energy
item.description: China has laid out broad new rules that mandate increased use of renewables as it continues to shift the focus of its energy transition from building new generation to ensuring it gets consumed.

item.title: Lithium Plunges in China on Speculation Over Status of CATL Mine
item.description: Chinese lithium futures have shed about 9% in two days on unconfirmed speculation one of the world’s biggest mines for the battery metal may restart soon.

item.title: SpaceX Shares Poised to Fall Again as US Market Reopens
item.description: SpaceX shares fell as much as 4.6% in premarket trading on Monday, putting the stock on pace for a third straight loss as markets reopened following a public holiday.

item.title: Pound Lower, Gilts Steady as UK Prime Minister Starmer Resigns
item.description: The pound held losses and gilts traded steady after UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced he will step down, with traders weighing whether a new Labour administration could revive concerns over the UK’s fiscal outlook.

item.title: Godiva Japan Said to Seek Extension on $464 Million LBO Loan
item.description: Godiva Japan Inc. is in talks with its banking syndicate to extend the repayment deadline on leveraged buyout financing as the chocolate maker struggles in the major Asian market, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Business Meets to Discuss Intervention in Johannesburg Crisis
item.description: Business Leadership South Africa, a lobby group that counts many of the chief executive officers of the country’s biggest companies among its members, said it will meet this week to discuss support for Johannesburg, which is struggling to pay its bills.

item.title: BOE Sets £40 Billion Stablecoin Cap and Drops Holding Limit
item.description: The Bank of England has scrapped plans to limit how much firms and households can hold in UK stablecoins and instead introduced a temporary £40 billion ($52.8 billion) cap for the total issuance of each coin.

item.title: Australian Spot Power Price Jumps on Still and Cloudy Weather
item.description: A key spot price in Australia’s electricity market jumped as calm conditions curbed wind turbine output, highlighting weather’s growing role as a driver of power market volatility.

item.title: French, German Governments Agree to Hold Equal Stakes in KNDS
item.description: The German and French governments are set to hold equal stakes in KNDS NV, one of Europe’s most important defense companies, as it gears up for an initial public offering in the near future.

item.title: Europe’s Heat Wave Intensifies With France on Red Alert
item.description: A heat wave gripping western Europe is forecast to strengthen this week, with days of dangerously hot and humid conditions and record-breaking temperatures in France, the UK, Spain and Germany.

item.title: Grace Shao on What the World Should Know About Chinese AI
item.description: This is how the Chinese ecosystem is different.

item.title: Odd Lots: Grace Shao on How Chinese AI is Different (Podcast)
item.description: China’s AI industry has changed a lot since DeepSeek released its cheap frontier model last year, and briefly sent US tech stocks falling. After being locked out of the most advanced chips, Chinese companies are now allowed to buy some Nvidia H200s. In fact, many of the big Chinese tech companies — like Baidu — are making a push to become full-stack players, with their own chips, models, and cloud infrastructure. Today’s guest is Grace Shao, an independent AI researcher and the author of the AI

item.title: Escrivá Says ECB Must Be Vigilant on Oil Price Impact on Wages
item.description: The European Central Bank must watch for any knock-on impact on wages from higher oil and commodity prices resulting from the Middle East conflict, Governing Council member Jose Luis Escrivá said.

item.title: Chinese Backing Helps African Startup Spiro Near Unicorn Status
item.description: Chinese investor NewTrails Capital will invest $55 million into African electric mobility startup Spiro, helping boost the company’s valuation.

item.title: Southern Water Gets £300m Equity as Asterion Joins Macquarie
item.description: Southern Water Ltd. reached a target to raise £1.2 billion ($1.6 billion) in equity to fund major network upgrades after securing the final tranche of investment from a new shareholder.

item.title: European Gas Edges Higher After Rocky Start to Iran Peace Talks
item.description: European natural gas edged higher after talks between the US and Iran appeared to get off to a shaky start, with President Donald Trump issuing fresh threats even as discussions on a peace deal proceed.

item.title: Bond Traders Finally Get Single View of Activity in UK Market
item.description: Bond investors gained access to a single stream of UK trading data after a transparency initiative that’s been years in the making went live.

item.title: Chinese Steel Market Enters Long Plateau After Property Crash
item.description: The Chinese steel market is navigating a prolonged weakening in demand rather than a cliff-drop collapse, as the steep decline from property is increasingly offset by manufacturing and exports, according to experts at an industry conference last week.

item.title: Peer-Beating Tech Fund Doubles China Exposure in Contrarian Bet
item.description: A peer-beating technology stock fund is taking profits on some hot US trades and adding exposure in China, seeing opportunities in the Asian nation’s internet giants and hardware makers.

item.title: Iranian Crude Prices Slashed as More Shipments Exit Hormuz
item.description: Sellers of Iranian crude to China have slashed prices after the Islamic Republic started shipping out millions of barrels following the interim peace deal that with the US.

item.title: EasyJet Rejects Three Separate Overtures From Castlelake
item.description: EasyJet Plc has rejected three separate offers from Castlelake LP, prompting the investment firm to take its latest proposal that values the budget carrier at about £4.74 billion ($6.3 billion) directly to shareholders.
